commit.message:
Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update FCC address collection and add fcc-ip-doc-updater agent

Add fcc-ip-doc-updater agent for incremental FCC address updates from
GitHub issue #5 community feedback, with strict deletion policy and
address ordering rules. Update FCC address docs with 8 new addresses
(Zhejiang Wenzhou 4, Hunan Shaoyang 1, Henan Unicom 2, Hebei Telecom 1).

commit.message:
v1.22.74: Diagnostic — flip sentinel to PAGE_NOACCESS after 10 zero-page hits

After the 10th zero-page VEH redirect, temporarily set the sentinel page
to PAGE_NOACCESS. This forces every subsequent access to fault and be
logged, revealing the tight loop's access pattern that causes the New
Game freeze. After 500 faults, restore PAGE_READWRITE. Log limit raised
to 600 to capture the full diagnostic window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: SSE Engine Fixes for Wine — Wine/Proton/CrossOver-compatible fork of SSE Engine Fixes by aers

commit.message:
fix(engine): disable prepared statements in pool to fix DuplicatePreparedStatement

prepare_threshold=0 caused every statement to be prepared, leading to
conflicts across pool connections. None disables prepared statements entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix is_frame_different() to compare address column, not just byte data

Previously, scrolling through all-FF memory regions caused frames to be
skipped as duplicates because only the byte data region was compared.
Now also compares the address column (threshold 5000) so address changes
are detected even when byte content is identical.

commit.message:
Agent session bootstrap, AGENT.md convention, and git discipline hooks (#2)

* add agent session bootstrap guide for all major vendors

Documents how to configure Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Windsurf,
Copilot, Aider, Cline, and Devin to read the Calypso blueprint and project
PRD at the start of every session. Introduces AGENT.md as the vendor-agnostic
source of truth and a deploy script that installs it in the correct path and
format per vendor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add implementation plan convention and pre-commit enforcement hook

Introduces docs/plans/implementation-plan.md as a living checklist of
agent work tasks, distinct from the PRD. Adds a blocking pre-commit hook
that rejects any commit that does not stage an update to the plan, ensuring
the agent's task state is always current. Documents bootstrapping the hook
via scripts/hooks/pre-commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refine commit metadata schema and add commit-msg enforcement hook

Sharpens the GIT_BRAIN_METADATA schema semantics: the retroactive_prompt
field is now explicitly defined as the instruction you would write after
completing the task — not the original prompt — forming a causal chain of
informed decisions across commits. Adds a commit-msg hook that deterministically
blocks commits with a missing block, invalid JSON, absent required fields, or
a retroactive_prompt under 50 characters. Validation logic is extracted into
scripts/hooks/validate-commit-metadata.mjs for testability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* introduce next-prompt.md as agent state machine cursor

Adds docs/plans/next-prompt.md as a third planning document alongside the
implementation plan. Each agent session reads it at startup and executes it
autonomously; each session overwrites it with the next session's instruction
before committing. This closes the loop between sessions — the agent can
advance without a human prompt. Enforced by the pre-commit hook, which now
blocks commits that do not stage both planning files. AGENT.md template
updated to include all four session-start reads and the commit requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* correct terminology: commit is the unit of work, not session

A Calypso commit represents one completed task. An agent session spans many
commits. next-prompt.md is written at each commit for the next commit, not
for the next session. Updated all language in the blueprint, git-standards,
and agent bootstrap guide to reflect this distinction precisely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* clarify pre-flight hook behaviour: blocks, warnings, and test annotation

Pre-commit blocks only on missing planning docs (implementation-plan.md,
next-prompt.md). Lint/format run as advisory warnings that do not block
the commit but remind the agent to include fixes in next-prompt.md.
Pre-push blocks on lint/format/type failures. Full test suite runs on
every push; failures do not block the push but are appended to
next-prompt.md with a mandatory instruction to fix or rewrite them —
never ignore or skip. Documents the distinction clearly in a summary
table at the top of the Pre-flight section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* pre-commit runs auto-fix then flags unfixable issues into next-prompt.md

At commit time, eslint --fix and prettier --write run first to silently
repair what they can. The hook then re-checks for surviving issues.
Anything that could not be auto-fixed is explicitly appended to
next-prompt.md so the agent addresses it in the next commit. The commit
is not blocked. The summary table is updated to reflect this distinction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* redesign AGENT.md template: calypso redux, task loop, antipatterns

Replaces the verbose AGENT.md template with a structured three-part layout:
(1) Calypso Redux — tight bullet summary of the method covering the commit
state machine, TDD, no-mock policy, fixed stack, bare metal, and metadata;
(2) Task Start — @import-based reads of implementation-plan and next-prompt
with IMPORTANT directive to execute immediately; (3) Antipatterns — explicit
list of thirteen common agent defaults that Calypso forbids, including mocking,
fabricated fixtures, skipping tests, ORMs, Docker, external auth, headed
browsers, and incorrect retroactive prompt writing. Adds note on @import
support per vendor and Codex limitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* enforce commit frequency and PR size limits via hooks and antipatterns

Pre-commit hook warns (non-blocking) when a commit touches more than 10
files excluding planning docs, appending a note to next-prompt.md.
Pre-push hook blocks if the PR exceeds 30 files changed vs. main, with
instructions to split the branch into smaller PRs. Summary table updated.
Two new antipatterns added to AGENT.md: batching too much into one commit,
and accumulating large PRs. Thresholds: 10 files per commit, 30 files per PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* expand AGENT.md template: philosophy, standards reference, new antipatterns

Adds @imports for all four standards documents so agents load them into
context automatically. Adds a Philosophy section listing the prior art
Calypso subscribes to (TDD, YAGNI, UNIX philosophy, Choose Boring
Technology, Worse is Better, Locality of Behaviour, Risk-First, 12-Factor)
and explicitly does not subscribe to (microservices by default, resume-
driven development, DRY at all costs, GitFlow, BDUF, abstraction-first,
framework layering). Adds a Standards Reference table linking every
document to its topic. Adds two new antipatterns: TypeScript only (no
other languages), and bun only (no npm/npx). Adds antipattern against
jumping straight to implementation without planning and stubbing first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* compress AGENT.md template for token efficiency

Reduces the template from ~110 lines to ~40. Key cuts: philosophy
collapsed from 15 bulleted items to two dense single lines; standards
reference table removed (redundant with @imports); antipatterns
consolidated from 18 verbose bullets to 6 grouped one-liners under
a Do Not section; method summary tightened from 11 to 9 bullets with
prose justifications stripped. All rules preserved, prose removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* add post-commit hook for cumulative PR readiness warning

Adds a post-commit hook that counts total files changed on the current
branch vs. main after every commit. When the count reaches 10, it prints
a boxed console warning and appends a PR instruction to next-prompt.md.
This sits between the per-commit size advisory (>10 files staged) and the
pre-push hard block (>30 files) — it is the early signal that enough
change has accumulated to form a reviewable PR. Bootstrap command updated
to include post-commit alongside pre-commit, commit-msg, and pre-push.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* lower PR file limit from 30 to 20

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: support ZIP archive uploads and fix E2E tests (#78)

* feat: support uploading ZIP archives containing ROM files

Extract ZIP files server-side during upload, stage each ROM individually,
and silently ignore non-ROM files (.txt, .nfo, etc.). Inner .zip files
are treated as ROM files for libretro compatibility.

Includes zip bomb protection (50GB cumulative limit, 10k file cap),
path traversal prevention, and temp file cleanup on all code paths.

Also fixes Playwright E2E auth by wiring up the setup project with
storageState dependencies in playwright.config.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bot turn scheduling: call scheduleBotTurnIfNeeded in openMatchWindow

Also reduce bot delay from 5s to 3s.

commit.message:
Add data-driven developer clustering blog post

Replace fabricated archetypes with real clusters from survey data:
- K-means clustering on 7,041 JetBrains respondents (75 behavioral features)
- Cross-validated against 30,816 Stack Overflow respondents
- Both surveys independently converge on 3 clusters (silhouette 0.05/0.22)
- Claude Code users significantly over-represented in Heavy Delegators
  cluster (+14pp, chi2=65, p<0.001)
- 12 matplotlib figures, all login-node safe
- Personal usage data: 192 sessions, $1,046/15 days, projected into clusters

New files: cluster_analysis.py (preprocessing pipeline), clusters.json
(derived data), run_clustering.slurm, gen_thumbnail.py

commit.message:
fix: replace always() with !cancelled() in all workflows (#1873)

always() runs even when workflows are cancelled, risking hangs.
!cancelled() is the recommended alternative per GitHub docs — it
runs on success or failure but respects cancellation.

Inline single-line `if:` expressions use ${{ !cancelled() }} to
avoid YAML tag parsing issues. Multi-line blocks (| and >-) use
!cancelled() directly.

Fix Spline mouse events and Inter font (root cause)

  the Spline canvas area as an invisible interactive layer → add pointer-events-none
  to container, pointer-events-auto only on actual content
- Inter: move inter.variable from body to html element so --font-sans CSS var
  is available at root scope for all descendants

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 401 redirect breaking claim flow

The 401 response interceptor was redirecting to /vue/auth/login on any
unauthenticated API response. Since testnet_reward has no session token,
every claim attempt hit this redirect and landed on a non-existent page.

- Remove the window.location.href redirect from the 401 handler; let
  component-level error handling surface the error as a toast instead
- Pass email explicitly in the claim emit so handleClaimFromConnected
  always has it regardless of currentEmail state

commit.message:
fix: migration 0048 DROP PRIMARY KEY IF EXISTS → PREPARE/EXECUTE conditional

MariaDB 10.11 does not support the IF EXISTS clause for DROP PRIMARY KEY.
Use information_schema + PREPARE/EXECUTE to conditionally drop the PK
only when it exists, then re-add it cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Buddhi redirect patterns + blocked-tool tracking

Engine gap fixed: blocked tools (Lotus route miss, Iron Dome) now feed
into Buddhi evaluation alongside executed tools. Previously blocked tools
bypassed Buddhi entirely, so stuck patterns on invalid tools went undetected.

New redirect patterns (deterministic, fires before reflect):
- edit_file failing 2x with "not found" → redirect to read_file first
- write_file failing 2x → redirect to verify path with glob/read_file
- Repeated route misses → redirect with list of valid tool names

Engine now handles redirect verdict (injects guidance like reflect).

239 tests pass.
